title: fractalart
text: This file will become your README and also the index of your documentation.

title: fractalart
text:
Developer Guide
If you are new to using nbdev here are some useful pointers to get you started.

Install fractalart in Development mode
# make sure fractalart package is installed in development mode
$ pip install -e .

# make changes under nbs/ directory
# ...

# compile to have changes apply to fractalart
$ nbdev_prepare

title: fractalart
text:
Usage

Installation
Install latest from the GitHub repository:
$ pip install git+https://github.com/eandreas/fractalart.git
or from conda
$ conda install -c eandreas fractalart
or from pypi
$ pip install fractalart


Documentation
Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub repository’s pages. Additionally you can find package manager specific guidelines on conda and pypi respectively.

title: fractal

source

Fractal

 Fractal (width:int=800, height:int=800, x_min:float=-2.0,
          x_max:float=1.0, y_min:float=-1.5, y_max:float=1.5,
          max_iter:int=1000)

Abstract base class for generating fractal images.

source


Mandelbrot

 Mandelbrot (width:int=800, height:int=800, x_min:float=-2.0,
             x_max:float=1.0, y_min:float=-1.5, y_max:float=1.5,
             max_iter:int=1000)

Abstract base class for generating fractal images.

source


Julia

 Julia (cr:float, ci:float, width:int=800, height:int=800,
        x_min:float=-1.5, x_max:float=1.5, y_min:float=-1.5,
        y_max:float=1.5, max_iter:int=1000)

Abstract base class for generating fractal images.




Type
Default

int
1000
keep same (or change)




j = Julia(cr = -0.7, ci = 0.27015)
j.resolution = 1200, 1200
j.max_iter = 3000
j.render()
j.equalize_histogram()
j.plot()

class Mandelbrot(Fractal):
    def compute(self) -> np.ndarray:
        w, h = self.resolution
        # pass resolution-consistent dims
        return _compute_mandelbrot_set(self._x_min, self._x_max, self._y_min, self._y_max, w, h, self._max_iter)
